- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

# Umberhold Lesser Magical Items

This document captures the current approved **Lesser** magical item list for Umberhold.

Magical items are grouped into three tiers:
- **Lesser**
- **Greater**
- **Exalted**

This file currently covers the **Lesser** tier only.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## General Notes

- weights use **stones**
- values are shown in **Gold Crowns** with legacy `gp` shorthand preserved in parentheses
- entries marked **Varies** inherit the underlying weapon or armor weight
- entries marked **Random** require item-generation tables that are not yet authored here

---

## Lesser Magical Item Table

| Item Name | Type | Weight | Value | Effect |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Potion of Healing** | Potion | 1 stone | 50 Gold Crowns (`50 gp`) | Heals **3d6 + 3 hit points** when consumed. |
| **Potion of Cleansing** | Potion | 1 stone | 100 Gold Crowns (`100 gp`) | Removes **all poisons and diseases** when consumed. |
| **Scroll of Random 1st-Level Spell** | Scroll | 1 stone | 75 Gold Crowns (`75 gp`) | Contains one **random 1st-level spell**. |
| **Scroll of Random 2nd-Level Spell** | Scroll | 1 stone | 150 Gold Crowns (`150 gp`) | Contains one **random 2nd-level spell**. |
| **Random +1 Weapon** | Weapon | Varies | 500 Gold Crowns (`500 gp`) | Grants **+1 to attack and damage rolls**. Weapon type is determined randomly. |
| **Random +1 Light Armor** | Armor (Body) | Varies | 400 Gold Crowns (`400 gp`) | Grants **+1 AC**. Light armor type is determined randomly. |
| **Random +1 Medium Armor** | Armor (Body) | Varies | 600 Gold Crowns (`600 gp`) | Grants **+1 AC**. Medium armor type is determined randomly. |
| **+1 Shield** | Shield (Off-hand) | 6 stones | 500 Gold Crowns (`500 gp`) | Grants **+1 AC**. |
| **Potion of Dragon's Breath** | Potion | 1 stone | 150 Gold Crowns (`150 gp`) | Breathe on one enemy at **range 1**. The breath hits the target and any adjacent enemies on either side, dealing **3d8 elemental damage**. **DC 15 Agility save** for half damage. Comes in four variants: **Red** (fire), **Black** (acid), **Blue** (lightning), and **White** (cold). |
| **Potion of Marrow Muncher Strength** | Potion | 1 stone | 200 Gold Crowns (`200 gp`) | Strength becomes **18 for 1 hour**. A Marrow Muncher is a hairy, ogre-like creature. |
| **Gloves of the Black Hand** | Gloves (Hands) | 1 stone | 250 Gold Crowns (`250 gp`) | Grants **+4 to Lockpicking and Remove Traps checks**. |
| **Gauntlets of the Marrow Muncher** | Gloves (Hands) | 2 stones | 600 Gold Crowns (`600 gp`) | Grants **Strength 18** while worn. |
| **Mithral Chain Shirt** | Armor (Body) | 5 stones | 800 Gold Crowns (`800 gp`) | Functions as a chain shirt; weight is reduced to **5 stones** and it imposes **no Agility modifier penalty**. |
| **Zarveth's Shifting Cloak** | Cloak (Back) | 1 stone | 700 Gold Crowns (`700 gp`) | A purple-blue patchwork cloak that shifts in the light. Grants **+2 AC**. Does **not** stack with similar spell effects. **Cannot be worn over armor**. |
| **Sunblade** | Weapon (Any Sword) | Varies | 600 Gold Crowns (`600 gp`) | The weapon is any sword, determined randomly. When equipped, it provides **magical illumination (3 squares)**. |
| **Ring of Feather Falling** | Ring (Finger) | no weight | 400 Gold Crowns (`400 gp`) | The wearer is **immune to falling damage**. |
| **Graveveil Cloak** | Cloak (Back) | 1 stone | 500 Gold Crowns (`500 gp`) | Made of shifting leather skinned from graveyard bodies. While worn, there is a **5% chance + Luck modifier** that damage which would reduce the wearer to **0 hit points** instead leaves them at **1 hit point**. When found, it has **2d4 + 2 charges**. |
| **Potion of Speed** | Potion | 1 stone | 250 Gold Crowns (`250 gp`) | Lasts **1 hour** and grants **advantage on Initiative rolls**. |
| **Woodland Bracers** | Gloves (Hands) | 1 stone | 350 Gold Crowns (`350 gp`) | Leather bracers tooled from woodland creatures. Grants **+2 to attack rolls with shortbow and longbow**. |
| **Cap of the Unbound Mind** | Cap (Head) | 1 stone | 300 Gold Crowns (`300 gp`) | The wearer has **advantage against spells and effects that would Beguile** them. |
| **Bonepick Key** | Key | no weight | 100 Gold Crowns (`100 gp`) | Carved from a thief's finger bone. Opens **one lock**, then crumbles to dust. |
| **Zarveth's Wand** | Wand (Held) | 1 stone | 650 Gold Crowns (`650 gp`) | A dark, root-like wand of twisted wood. When found, it has **4d10 charges**. **Hedge Wizard only.** It casts **Zarveth's Acid Darts**: target one enemy; make **three ranged spell attacks** (**d20 + Proficiency Bonus + Casting Bonus vs AC**). Each hit deals **1d4 + 1 poison** damage. If any attack is a natural 20, the target is **Poisoned** until the end of its next turn. |
| **Gravebreaker Mace** | Weapon (Mace) | 4 stones | 550 Gold Crowns (`550 gp`) | Deals an extra **2d6 holy damage** to undead. |
| **Shadowman's Blade** | Weapon (Katana) | 3 stones | 650 Gold Crowns (`650 gp`) | Grants **+1 to attack and damage rolls**. **Only when wielded by a Shadowman**, its hits cause **Bleeding**, dealing an extra damage die at the start of the target's next turn. |

---

## Implementation Notes

- **Scroll of Random 1st-Level Spell** and **Scroll of Random 2nd-Level Spell** still need their random spell tables.
- **Random +1 Weapon**, **Random +1 Light Armor**, **Random +1 Medium Armor**, **Sunblade**, and similar entries still need their subtype roll tables.
- **Graveveil Cloak** has a defined starting charge count, but the exact charge-consumption rule should be confirmed during implementation.
- **Zarveth's Wand** uses the already approved **Zarveth's Acid Darts** spell profile.

---

## Status

This file establishes the first authored tier of Umberhold magical items.
**Greater** and **Exalted** magical item lists still need to be created.

content.thinking:
**Considering user request**

The user asked for specific content, so I don’t need to create a broad index. I think it’s a good idea to note at the top-level `umberhold/README` that it doesn’t exist. Aligning with the existing save names is likely necessary for consistency. I want to make sure I’m being thorough while also keeping it straightforward for the user. Let's ensure everything is organized clearly!

**Drafting trap details**

I need to create an interesting list of traps, using spells, monster attacks, and existing data. I should have 8-10 traps for each list (A and B), and they should get progressively more lethal. Each trap needs a table with the name, target (like a character or the whole party), and effects, including any saving throws. It’s essential to reference existing spells and attacks, maybe even including some novel traps alongside more lethal designs. I'll aim for a dark or grim style!

**Structuring traps for gameplay**

I’m focusing on creating traps for two tiers: A for early-game but still nasty, and B for more lethal options. I think I’ll standardize saving throws, maybe aiming for DCs between 10-15 for consistency. There should also be party-wide area effects, with some potentially having no saves at all, like teleportation. I need to highlight lethal potential at the end of each list. I'm considering creating "Trap Tier A" and "Trap Tier B" tables, plus a chest table, along with design notes for future reference.

**Searching for trap inspiration**

I think I should consult monster attacks from other files for more trap ideas. I'm considering creatures like the wulf, rotwife, grave slurry, gleaming halberd, marrow muncher, and griselda. My goal is to find potential trap names and mechanics based on these. Let’s inspect these monsters quickly to see what inspiration I can gather! This could help enhance the creativity and variety of the traps I'm designing.

content.text:
# Boggart Rotwife

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Rotwife |
| **Description** | A hunched, string-haired crone daubed in ash, grease, and clotted herbs. Her skin hangs loose and mottled, marked with boils and ritual cuts, and her breath carries the sour stink of rot and old bile. Bone charms and fetishes clatter softly at her waist as she mutters to herself, eyes gleaming with spiteful cunning. She croons half-formed lullabies before unleashing foul magic, delighting in watching her victims falter and fade. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (base 10 + Agility + filthy wrappings) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 |
| **Strength** | 8 (-1) |
| **Constitution** | 10 (+0) |
| **Agility** | 12 (+1) |
| **Intellect** | 12 (+1) |
| **Piety** | 12 (+1) |
| **Personality** | 10 (+0) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | Piety +3 |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Crude Club:** +1 to hit, range 1, 1d6 - 1 bludgeoning damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife swings a crude club at (insert target)!* **Venomspit (Cantrip):** +3 to hit, range 3, 2d4 acid damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife spits a glob of hissing venom at (insert target)!* **Rotwife’s Lullaby (Recharge 5/6):** range 3; one enemy and up to one adjacent ally on each side in the same rank (3 max); targets must make a **PIE save** or fall asleep. Each target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Ends immediately for a target if it takes damage. Combat text: *Boggart Rotwife croons a foul lullaby at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | B; **Silver Unholy Symbol** (5%) |
| **Comments** | “Sleep now, little meat...”, “Close them eyes... never wake...”, “Heh... dream of rot...”, “Soft flesh, soft mind...”, “Hush now... hush...” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Traits

| Trait | Effect |
|---|---|
| None | None |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Rotwife has a chance to drop:
- **Silver Unholy Symbol** — 5%

### Silver Unholy Symbol Summary
A crude, tarnished icon etched with profane markings, sticky with old grime.
- mundane
- worth 25 gp

### Spell Note
**Rotwife’s Lullaby** affects one enemy and up to one adjacent ally on each side in the same rank, for a maximum of 3 targets. Sleeping targets may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns, and the effect ends immediately if a target takes damage.

# Grave Slurry

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Grave Slurry |
| **Description** | A heaving mass of liquefied corpses, grave soil, and splintered bone, slowly dragging itself forward in a wet, sucking crawl. Pale fragments of faces and limbs surface and sink within it, while jagged shards of bone jut outward like broken teeth. The air around it is thick with the stench of rot and churned earth, enough to turn the stomach before it ever strikes. |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Medium (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Undead, Ooze |
| **Alignment** | Neutral |
| **AC** | 10 |
| **Hit Points** | 2d8 + 2 |
| **Strength** | 12 (+1) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |
| **Agility** | 6 (-2) |
| **Intellect** | 1 (-5) |
| **Piety** | 4 (-3) |
| **Personality** | 1 (-5) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Pseudopod:** +3 to hit, range 1, 2d4 acid damage. A successful hit generates a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check, with a **DC of 5** *(see ITEM DESTRUCTION).* Combat text: *Grave Slurry lashes out with a sludge-thick pseudopod toward (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | None |
| **Comments** | Wet gurgling, bone grinding, low sucking noises |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Traits

| Trait | Effect |
|---|---|
| None | None |

---

## Notes

### Item Destruction Note
A successful **Pseudopod** hit generates a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check with a **DC of 5**.

### Encounter Note
Grave Slurries drag themselves through crypts, pits, and churned burial earth, fouling the ground as they advance.

# Rotwing

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Rotwing |

I think editing a file would be worthwhile since the request asks for an interesting list and tables. I'll create a markdown file with a clear structure, including a title like "Umberhold Treasure Chests and Trap Tables" and a description of the content I plan to cover.

**Creating treasure and trap tables**

I’m planning to include tiers C-E and treasure beyond tier B later, with a dark and grim style throughout. I’ll replicate the user’s chest table format, possibly naming it "Treasure Table" or "Chest Table." For the Trap Tier A and B tables, I’ll include columns for d10, Trap Name, Target, and Effect. I think it’s important to note that trap tables will be ordered from least to most lethal. I’ll also mention that most traps are one-shot unless they transform the encounter. Finally, I could reference existing spells or monsters in the effect descriptions.

I’m thinking about whether to add an extra column called "Inspired By." The user didn’t ask for it, but it might help. For now, I’ll keep it minimal with four columns, and I can mention existing data in the effect text. The style needs to be dark and grim, so the names and descriptions should reflect that. For Trap Tier A, I'll include around 10 escalating traps, such as Grave-Dust Sneezers and Whisper Needle, considering their mechanics and impact on the party.

**Considering encounter progression**

The user wants progressively more lethal options for encounters. I think using the Taxman might break the progression if placed too low since it's nonlethal. It could work as a nuisance trap, perhaps positioned at #2 or #3, but I need to ensure it's balanced with gameplay stakes rather than just HP. The chest mimic sounds like a great addition for tension, with potential lethal effects. I should separate the lists into A for lower stakes and B for higher damage encounters. Let's brainstorm!

**Standardizing trap mechanics**

I need to focus on using current spells and monster attacks accurately. It makes sense to create unique trap names and effects based on these elements. Considering the user’s request, I should include saving throw DCs ranging from 10-13 for category A and 12-15 for category B. Save types could be AGI, CON, PIE, and STR; existing canon already uses PIE, AGI, and CON. Standardizing this across the traps will provide clarity and consistency. Let's refine that!

**Considering party dynamics**

I'm thinking about Rotwife's Croon and whether it affects the whole party or just the opener plus adjacent allies. Outside of combat, should it be the opener plus one adjacent rank since there are no ranks while exploring? If I consider the whole party, could that lead to problems if they fall asleep and an encounter follows? Hmm, but if there’s nothing after the chest screen, maybe it’s less risky.

**Considering game mechanics**

I think I need to add one B trap, maybe a teleport trap to the dungeon start. For example, I could use "The Long Walk Home" scenario—where the whole party gets teleported back to the dungeon entrance with no option to save. It wouldn’t be lethal, but it certainly could be devastating for the players! I’m curious to see how this could impact their strategy and the overall game experience.

**Considering game mechanics**

I’m reflecting on the difficulty of the game without HP loss; it's a strong design choice that also extinguishes non-magical light sources, adding a grim style. This choice might enhance resource danger while progressing through lethal encounters. I think I should develop trap tables with detailed columns for name, target, and effect, ordered from least to most lethal. Additionally, I'll consider updating the index files to keep things organized, but I wonder if it's necessary to move forward with that integration.

**Planning new documentation**

I need to consider if I should inspect `umberhold/canon.md` before deciding to add to it, but probably it’s simpler just to create a new file. I think a top-level path like `umberhold/treasure-chests-canon.md` would work well for including a chest table and trap tiers, with the option to add treasure tiers later. I'll use the existing canon style, creating sections for title, purpose, coverage notes, chest table, and trap design notes, reserving space for future additions on traps and treasures.

**Considering lock chances**

I could clarify that "Effects below draw from approved spells and monster attacks already written elsewhere." It seems I might need to include lock chances since the user’s table refers to `DC 5d4` and `DC 5d4 + 1`. However, the current chest canon uses difficulty labels instead of percentiles. Still, the user’s HTML table suggests a variant, so I think I should include a "Locked Chance" section with the DC as a dynamic value. This approach could enhance clarity for users.

**Designing chest variants**

It seems like I need to create some chest variants, like using "Trunk" or "Footlocker." I also want to include elements like "Trapped Chance" and "Trap Tier" with percentages. Maybe I should have a section called "Trap Tier A" and "Trap Tier B," where I roll a d10 for each, and I should order them from least to most lethal. I really need to write this carefully, maintaining consistency with existing saves and conditions while drafting the traps with balanced mechanics. Let’s get started!

**Creating grim effects**

To add a dark tone, I’m considering an effect where a character might "blind and cough blood," causing **1d4 poison damage** and blinding them until the end of their next turn if they fail the save. The second idea is the "Taxman in the Lock," where a spectral figure makes the whole party lose **10% of their gold**—definitely punishing but without saving. 

Third, I have the "Bell-Wire of Solace," giving a **DC 10 PIE save** to avoid **1d4 psychic damage** and a frightened condition if they fail by five or more. Lastly, I’ve noted the "Boneboiler’s Greeting," where a concealed grease ampoule can inflict fire damage and blindness with a successful save halving the damage.

**Describing trap effects**

I'm thinking about the "Rotgas Latch," which involves boggart gas trapped under a lid. Each party member must make a **DC 10 CON save** or suffer **1d4 poison damage** and become **Poisoned**. If they fail by five or more, they also become **Blinded**. I might want to allow for a success to result in halved damage or no conditions at all. This will utilize the gasser mechanic. 

I’ve also got a concept called "Hook in the Velvet," where a spring hook tears into a character's hand or wrist. That sounds pretty intense!

**Figuring out party mechanics**

I’m considering how to handle the mechanics for Rotmother's Belch Jar. It looks like each character or the whole party must make a DC 12 agility save or take 2d6 acid damage, halving it if they save. That makes sense! Then I’m thinking about the Judgment of the False Reliquary — should it apply to the whole party or just an individual character? It seems like I need to clarify that before proceeding.

**Organizing game elements**

I'm noting that I could include treasure that gets recovered after defeating the mimic, which sounds good. I'm thinking about adding "Teleportation to start" and maybe "Taxman" in section B. I want to place "The Long Walk Home" at #5 in B since it's not lethal, but quite impactful. It seems good to maybe include "Taxman" at #2 in section A. I'm also considering creating "Trap Table A (roll d10)" and "Trap Table B (roll d10)."

**Clarifying content plans**

The user mentioned they don't need traps and treasure beyond A-B right now. They asked for trap lists, but there’s also a chest table with treasure types A-E. I guess I should note that treasure tables A-B will be authored later. Perhaps I can clarify in the "Current Coverage" section that this file focuses on trap tables A-B only. It seems best to create a standalone file rather than modifying a larger document. Adding a link to the README could help too!

**Producing a new README**

I’m considering creating a `umberhold/README` file. I could update `umberhold/list.md` or `canon.md`, but those might be too much to change. Since the file path is sufficient, I think it’s best to write a new file instead. I don’t need to read from a nonexistent file, so maybe I can write directly. I’ll take my time crafting the content carefully. Let’s get started on that!

arguments.content:
# Umberhold Treasure Chests and Trap Tables

This document captures the current approved working structure for **Treasure Chests** in Umberhold.

It currently defines:
- chest types **A-E**
- lock and trap chances by chest type
- authored trap tables for **Trap Tier A** and **Trap Tier B**

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. General Notes

- chest types are ordered from **A** to **E**
- higher chest types are more likely to be **locked** and **trapped**
- if a chest is trapped, it rolls on a trap tier based on its chest type
- for now, only **Trap Tier A** and **Trap Tier B** are authored here
- entries in each trap table are ordered from **least** to **most lethal** for easier curation
- if a trap transforms into a monster encounter, the treasure is recovered normally after the creature is defeated

---

## 2. Chest Table

| Type | Container Variants | Locked Chance | Trapped Chance | Trap Tier (if trapped) | Treasure |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Trunk / Footlocker | **20%** (`DC 5d4`) | **10%** | **85% A, 15% B** | **Chest A** |
| **B** | Strongbox / Iron Coffer | **40%** (`DC 5d4 + 1`) | **25%** | **15% A, 70% B, 15% C** | **Chest B** |
| **C** | Reinforced Chest / Merchant Chest | **60%** (`DC 5d4 + 3`) | **40%** | **15% B, 70% C, 15% D** | **Chest C** |
| **D** | Ornate Chest / Gilded Coffer | **80%** (`DC 5d4 + 5`) | **60%** | **15% C, 70% D, 15% E** | **Chest D** |
| **E** | Jeweled Reliquary / Royal Chest | **95%** (`DC 5d4 + 7`) | **75%** | **15% D, 85% E** | **Chest E** |

---

## 3. Trap Tier A

These are cruel but mostly early-game chest hazards. They are meant to wound, panic, separate, or punish greed without making every trap an automatic death sentence.

| # | Trap Name | Target | Effect |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Ashen Sneeze-Wire** | Character opening | A puff of grave-dust bursts into the eyes and lungs. **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the condition. |
| 2 | **Taxman in the Lock** | Whole party | A gaunt, smiling revenant tallies the party's wealth with a bone stylus, then takes its due. **No save.** The party loses **10% of its carried gold**, rounded up. The chest may still be opened afterward if no other hazard remains. |
| 3 | **Bell-Wire of Solace** | Character opening | A tiny funeral bell tolls inside the skull. **DC 10 Piety save** or take **1d4 psychic** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, the target is also **Frightened** until the end of its next turn. |
| 4 | **Boneboiler's Greeting** | Character opening | A hidden bladder of hot grease bursts across the face and hands. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **2d4 fire** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |
| 5 | **Rotgas Latch** | Whole party | The lid exhales a sealed cloud of corpse-green boggart gas. Each party member must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Poisoned**. If a save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid both conditions. |
| 6 | **Hook in the Velvet** | Character opening | A spring-hook punches through the wrist and tears free. **DC 11 Agility save** or take **1d6 piercing** damage and gain **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid **Bleeding**. |
| 7 | **Thornburst Hinge** | Whole party | Blackened barbs and root-thorns explode outward from the chest seams. Each party member must make a **DC 11 Agility save** or take **2d4 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 8 | **Rotwing Nest** | Character opening | A starving rotwing bursts from a false coin layer and drives its proboscis deep. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **1d4 + 2 piercing** damage and gain **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of the target's next turn. If the hit lands, the target must then make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or contract **Veinrot** for **1 hour**. While affected by **Veinrot**, the target takes **50% more damage** from **Bleeding** effects. |
| 9 | **Gloamshadow Seal** | Character opening | A hand of cold dusk closes over the heart. **DC 11 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d2 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 10 | **Hungry Lid** | Character opening, then whole party | The chest splits along wet seams and becomes a **Chest Mimic**. The opener is immediately targeted by its first bite: **+5 to hit**, **1d10 + 3 piercing** damage, and the target must make a **DC 13 Constitution save** or become **Paralyzed**. After that strike, combat begins. The treasure is recovered if the mimic is slain. |

---

## 4. Trap Tier B

These traps are meant for stronger parties and nastier places. They lean harder into burst damage, permanent pressure, and effects that can turn a successful expedition into a crawl back to safety.

| # | Trap Name | Target | Effect |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Venomspit Ampoule** | Character opening | A glass ampoule snaps under the latch and spits corrosive venom upward. **DC 11 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 2 | **Poppet's Kiss** | Character opening | A spring-loaded needle punches into the flesh with doll-like precision. The target takes **1 piercing** damage, then must make a **DC 12 Constitution save** or take **2d4 poison** damage. On a success, the poison damage is halved. |
| 3 | **Rotgas Reliquary** | Whole party | A buried censer cracks open and floods the space with embalmed poison fog. Each party member must make a **DC 11 Constitution save** or take **1d6 poison** damage and become **Poisoned**. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the conditions. |
| 4 | **Arcbolt Cage** | Character opening | Copper wire and damp iron teeth snap shut in a crackling halo. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **3d4 lightning** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 5 | **The Long Walk Home** | Whole party | The chest gives a single, hateful click. The floor, walls, and air fold like rotten cloth, and the party is hurled back to the **entrance or start** of the current dungeon. **No save.** All **non-magical light sources** currently burning are extinguished. |
| 6 | **Grave Slurry Reservoir** | Character opening | A hidden sac of corpse-sludge bursts over the arms and gear. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage and suffer a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check with **DC 5**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the item check. |
| 7 | **Gloamshadow Hand** | Character opening | A black, freezing grasp reaches out from inside the coffer and drinks the target's strength. **DC 12 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d3 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 8 | **Rotmother's Belch Jar** | Whole party | A sealed crock of bile, grease, and half-digested filth detonates across the party. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d6 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 9 | **Thorn-Coffin Mechanism** | Whole party | The chest blossoms into a cage of iron roots and inward-pointing thorns. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 + 2 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 10 | **Judgment of the False Reliquary** | Whole party | The chest erupts in a merciless pillar of pale, blinding light. Each party member must make a **DC 13 Piety save** or take **3d8 holy** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |

---

## 5. Authoring Notes

The following trap entries deliberately draw on already-authored Umberhold material, including:
- **Deathknell of Solace**
- **Arcbolt**
- **Thornburst**
- **Judgment of Solace**
- **Boggart Gasser** rotgas
- **Boggart Boneboiler** hot grease
- **Poppet of Pins** poison needle
- **Rotwing** bleeding and **Veinrot**
- **Gloamshadow** Strength-drain touch
- **Chest Mimic**
- **Grave Slurry** acid and item corrosion
- **Griselda Rotmother** bile belch

---

## 6. Still To Be Authored Later

- **Trap Tier C**
- **Trap Tier D**
- **Trap Tier E**
- **Treasure Table A**
- **Treasure Table B**
- later treasure tables beyond **B**

---

## 7. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone chest-and-trap reference sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

content.partialJson:
{"path":"umberhold/treasure-chests-canon.md","content":"# Umberhold Treasure Chests and Trap Tables

This document captures the current approved working structure for **Treasure Chests** in Umberhold.

It currently defines:
- chest types **A-E**
- lock and trap chances by chest type
- authored trap tables for **Trap Tier A** and **Trap Tier B**

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. General Notes

- chest types are ordered from **A** to **E**
- higher chest types are more likely to be **locked** and **trapped**
- if a chest is trapped, it rolls on a trap tier based on its chest type
- for now, only **Trap Tier A** and **Trap Tier B** are authored here
- entries in each trap table are ordered from **least** to **most lethal** for easier curation
- if a trap transforms into a monster encounter, the treasure is recovered normally after the creature is defeated

---

## 2. Chest Table

| Type | Container Variants | Locked Chance | Trapped Chance | Trap Tier (if trapped) | Treasure |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Trunk / Footlocker | **20%** (`DC 5d4`) | **10%** | **85% A, 15% B** | **Chest A** |
| **B** | Strongbox / Iron Coffer | **40%** (`DC 5d4 + 1`) | **25%** | **15% A, 70% B, 15% C** | **Chest B** |
| **C** | Reinforced Chest / Merchant Chest | **60%** (`DC 5d4 + 3`) | **40%** | **15% B, 70% C, 15% D** | **Chest C** |
| **D** | Ornate Chest / Gilded Coffer | **80%** (`DC 5d4 + 5`) | **60%** | **15% C, 70% D, 15% E** | **Chest D** |
| **E** | Jeweled Reliquary / Royal Chest | **95%** (`DC 5d4 + 7`) | **75%** | **15% D, 85% E** | **Chest E** |

---

## 3. Trap Tier A

These are cruel but mostly early-game chest hazards. They are meant to wound, panic, separate, or punish greed without making every trap an automatic death sentence.

| # | Trap Name | Target | Effect |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Ashen Sneeze-Wire** | Character opening | A puff of grave-dust bursts into the eyes and lungs. **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the condition. |
| 2 | **Taxman in the Lock** | Whole party | A gaunt, smiling revenant tallies the party's wealth with a bone stylus, then takes its due. **No save.** The party loses **10% of its carried gold**, rounded up. The chest may still be opened afterward if no other hazard remains. |
| 3 | **Bell-Wire of Solace** | Character opening | A tiny funeral bell tolls inside the skull. **DC 10 Piety save** or take **1d4 psychic** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, the target is also **Frightened** until the end of its next turn. |
| 4 | **Boneboiler's Greeting** | Character opening | A hidden bladder of hot grease bursts across the face and hands. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **2d4 fire** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |
| 5 | **Rotgas Latch** | Whole party | The lid exhales a sealed cloud of corpse-green boggart gas. Each party member must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Poisoned**. If a save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid both conditions. |
| 6 | **Hook in the Velvet** | Character opening | A spring-hook punches through the wrist and tears free. **DC 11 Agility save** or take **1d6 piercing** damage and gain **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid **Bleeding**. |
| 7 | **Thornburst Hinge** | Whole party | Blackened barbs and root-thorns explode outward from the chest seams. Each party member must make a **DC 11 Agility save** or take **2d4 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 8 | **Rotwing Nest** | Character opening | A starving rotwing bursts from a false coin layer and drives its proboscis deep. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **1d4 + 2 piercing** damage and gain **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of the target's next turn. If the hit lands, the target must then make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or contract **Veinrot** for **1 hour**. While affected by **Veinrot**, the target takes **50% more damage** from **Bleeding** effects. |
| 9 | **Gloamshadow Seal** | Character opening | A hand of cold dusk closes over the heart. **DC 11 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d2 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 10 | **Hungry Lid** | Character opening, then whole party | The chest splits along wet seams and becomes a **Chest Mimic**. The opener is immediately targeted by its first bite: **+5 to hit**, **1d10 + 3 piercing** damage, and the target must make a **DC 13 Constitution save** or become **Paralyzed**. After that strike, combat begins. The treasure is recovered if the mimic is slain. |

---

## 4. Trap Tier B

These traps are meant for stronger parties and nastier places. They lean harder into burst damage, permanent pressure, and effects that can turn a successful expedition into a crawl back to safety.

| # | Trap Name | Target | Effect |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Venomspit Ampoule** | Character opening | A glass ampoule snaps under the latch and spits corrosive venom upward. **DC 11 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 2 | **Poppet's Kiss** | Character opening | A spring-loaded needle punches into the flesh with doll-like precision. The target takes **1 piercing** damage, then must make a **DC 12 Constitution save** or take **2d4 poison** damage. On a success, the poison damage is halved. |
| 3 | **Rotgas Reliquary** | Whole party | A buried censer cracks open and floods the space with embalmed poison fog. Each party member must make a **DC 11 Constitution save** or take **1d6 poison** damage and become **Poisoned**. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the conditions. |
| 4 | **Arcbolt Cage** | Character opening | Copper wire and damp iron teeth snap shut in a crackling halo. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **3d4 lightning** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 5 | **The Long Walk Home** | Whole party | The chest gives a single, hateful click. The floor, walls, and air fold like rotten cloth, and the party is hurled back to the **entrance or start** of the current dungeon. **No save.** All **non-magical light sources** currently burning are extinguished. |
| 6 | **Grave Slurry Reservoir** | Character opening | A hidden sac of corpse-sludge bursts over the arms and gear. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage and suffer a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check with **DC 5**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the item check. |
| 7 | **Gloamshadow Hand** | Character opening | A black, freezing grasp reaches out from inside the coffer and drinks the target's strength. **DC 12 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d3 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 8 | **Rotmother's Belch Jar** | Whole party | A sealed crock of bile, grease, and half-digested filth detonates across the party. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d6 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 9 | **Thorn-Coffin Mechanism** | Whole party | The chest blossoms into a cage of iron roots and inward-pointing thorns. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 + 2 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 10 | **Judgment of the False Reliquary** | Whole party | The chest erupts in a merciless pillar of pale, blinding light. Each party member must make a **DC 13 Piety save** or take **3d8 holy** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |

---

## 5. Authoring Notes

The following trap entries deliberately draw on already-authored Umberhold material, including:
- **Deathknell of Solace**
- **Arcbolt**
- **Thornburst**
- **Judgment of Solace**
- **Boggart Gasser** rotgas
- **Boggart Boneboiler** hot grease
- **Poppet of Pins** poison needle
- **Rotwing** bleeding and **Veinrot**
- **Gloamshadow** Strength-drain touch
- **Chest Mimic**
- **Grave Slurry** acid and item corrosion
- **Griselda Rotmother** bile belch

---

## 6. Still To Be Authored Later

- **Trap Tier C**
- **Trap Tier D**
- **Trap Tier E**
- **Treasure Table A**
- **Treasure Table B**
- later treasure tables beyond **B**

---

## 7. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone chest-and-trap reference sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

content.text:
Created `umberhold/treasure-chests-canon.md`

What’s in it:
- Chest type table A-E
- 10 traps for **Trap Tier A**
- 10 traps for **Trap Tier B**
- Dark/grim tone consistent with the rest of Umberhold
- Effects tied to existing Umberhold material like:
  - **Arcbolt**
  - **Thornburst**
  - **Judgment of Solace**
  - **Boggart Gasser** rotgas
  - **Boneboiler** hot grease
  - **Poppet of Pins**
  - **Rotwing**
  - **Gloamshadow**
  - **Chest Mimic**
  - **Grave Slurry**
  - **Griselda Rotmother**

Included examples you mentioned:
- **Taxman in the Lock**
- **The Long Walk Home** (teleport to dungeon start)
- **Hungry Lid** / chest mimic

I kept the tables ordered from less lethal to more lethal within each tier, with several entries becoming genuinely dangerous or potentially fatal near the bottom.

If you want, I can do either of these next:
1. author **Treasure Table A** and **Treasure Table B**
2. author **Trap Tier C** next in the same style

You'll need to redo some of these - there's no point having a trap like the Ashen Sneeze-Wire that blinds the target until the end of its next turn - we're not in combat so it's irrelevant and not meaningful.  Change these to be at least 1 minute. And let's change all the Solace wording to the god of Darkness; Umbraxis.

content.thinking:
**Updating trap effects duration**

I’m focusing on updating the blindness durations for traps since they’re usually triggered outside of combat. The user suggests a minimum of 1 minute for statuses, which makes sense. I should consider which effects need this adjustment, like being blinded from various traps. It seems that any “until end of next turn” conditions are irrelevant in non-combat scenarios, so I’ll likely change those durations to at least 1 minute. However, effects from mimics can stay since they’re combat triggered.

**Clarifying trap status durations**

I’m thinking about how the whole party being blinded until the end of next turn is irrelevant if combat doesn’t happen, so I should update that to a duration of 1 minute. The Frightened status from the bell-wire might also be irrelevant. I’m unsure about the Poisoned duration—some attacks allow repeated saves to end the condition, but traps don’t indicate a duration. Since Rotgas Latch states it's Poisoned without duration, maybe I could assign 10 minutes for traps outside combat even though the user didn’t specifically request it.

**Considering trap durations**

I'm thinking about how to handle the durations for effects like blindness. If something's indefinite, it might be problematic. I wonder if we should set a minimum duration, maybe 10 minutes? It seems reasonable since the player highlighted the irrelevance of effects when not in combat. I'm also considering how to adapt trap entries to reflect meaningful impacts during exploration. This way, even if there are repeated saves, the effects have some value for the players outside of combat.

**Revising trap durations**

I think durations for effects in the trap table should be set to at least 1 minute. For example, effects that currently indicate being blinded until the end of a target's next turn might be adjusted to 1 minute or even 10 minutes for clarity. I’m considering making Poisoned effects last 10 minutes to give them some meaningful impact. Overall, I want to ensure that all combat-turn-based effects are consistent in duration, making them relevant for gameplay.

**Considering thornburst hinge mechanics**

I’m thinking about the Thornburst hinge and its bleeding effect, which applies next turn. It seems okay for immediate damage-over-time, but outside of combat, the "start of next turn" wording feels odd. Should I change the bleeding damage to happen right after 1 round, or is it fine as is? It might be relevant shortly after the trap triggers, even if it’s a bit awkward to say “at the end of the next 6 seconds.” Maybe leaving it as immediate follow-up damage makes the most sense.

**Considering broader changes**

I see that we've only changed the wording in this specific document regarding Solace, but the user mentioned they might want a project-wide rename. They specifically mentioned traps but also talked about changing all mentions of Solace to "Umbraxis." I think it would be a good idea to check the entire repository for occurrences of Solace since it sounds like a larger change might be needed. I need to determine the full scope of this, especially since many spells and items are Solace-themed. I'll use bash grep to help with this search.

**Searching for occurrences**

I need to make sure to follow the instructions to read before making any edits. It sounds like using bash for the grep command is the way to go. I’ll search for all occurrences of "Solace" to see where they appear in the code. However, effects from mimics can stay since they’re combat triggered."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Clarifying trap status durations**

I’m thinking about how the whole party being blinded until the end of next turn is irrelevant if combat doesn’t happen, so I should update that to a duration of 1 minute. The Frightened status from the bell-wire might also be irrelevant. I’m unsure about the Poisoned duration—some attacks allow repeated saves to end the condition, but traps don’t indicate a duration. Since Rotgas Latch states it's Poisoned without duration, maybe I could assign 10 minutes for traps outside combat even though the user didn’t specifically request it."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering trap durations**

I'm thinking about how to handle the durations for effects like blindness. If something's indefinite, it might be problematic. I wonder if we should set a minimum duration, maybe 10 minutes? It seems reasonable since the player highlighted the irrelevance of effects when not in combat. I'm also considering how to adapt trap entries to reflect meaningful impacts during exploration. This way, even if there are repeated saves, the effects have some value for the players outside of combat."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Revising trap durations**

I think durations for effects in the trap table should be set to at least 1 minute. For example, effects that currently indicate being blinded until the end of a target's next turn might be adjusted to 1 minute or even 10 minutes for clarity. I’m considering making Poisoned effects last 10 minutes to give them some meaningful impact. Overall, I want to ensure that all combat-turn-based effects are consistent in duration, making them relevant for gameplay."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering thornburst hinge mechanics**

I’m thinking about the Thornburst hinge and its bleeding effect, which applies next turn. It seems okay for immediate damage-over-time, but outside of combat, the "start of next turn" wording feels odd. Should I change the bleeding damage to happen right after 1 round, or is it fine as is? It might be relevant shortly after the trap triggers, even if it’s a bit awkward to say “at the end of the next 6 seconds.” Maybe leaving it as immediate follow-up damage makes the most sense."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering broader changes**

I see that we've only changed the wording in this specific document regarding Solace, but the user mentioned they might want a project-wide rename. They specifically mentioned traps but also talked about changing all mentions of Solace to "Umbraxis." I think it would be a good idea to check the entire repository for occurrences of Solace since it sounds like a larger change might be needed. I need to determine the full scope of this, especially since many spells and items are Solace-themed. I'll use bash grep to help with this search."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Searching for occurrences**

I need to make sure to follow the instructions to read before making any edits. It sounds like using bash for the grep command is the way to go. I’ll search for all occurrences of "Solace" to see where they appear in the code. This will help me determine the scope of any potential changes needed, especially if the user wants to do a broader project-wide rename. Let’s get started on that grep search!"}]}

content.thinking:
**Considering global changes**

The user might only want updates to the chest doc, but their request to "change all the Solace wording" suggests a broader change. Given the many references in the text, it could be wise to ask for clarification before doing a global replacement. However, if they prefer a global renaming, I need to be careful—certain phrases reflect significant lore changes. Automatic adjustments may work if they're switching to a god of Darkness, but it could get tricky with existing divine spells. I might suggest doing a broader pass later.

**Clarifying the update request**

I’m considering mentioning that I’ve updated the trap file, but there are many other references to Solace in the repo. The user said "all the Solace wording," which makes me think they may want global changes. I’ll need to carefully check if I can edit the chest doc directly. It might be best to clarify their intent first. They usually want things done quickly, so I could offer to scope my changes to this document while noting the broader references for future updates. A global rename could be significant.

**Considering name changes**

I need to think about renaming "Bell-Wire of Solace" to "Bell-Wire of Umbraxis" and updating authoring notes to reflect this. For "Judgment of the False Reliquary," maybe it can stay as it is if it doesn’t contain Solace wording. If I change everything to Umbraxis, I'd have to rename other spells to "Deathknell of Umbraxis" and "Judgment of Umbraxis." However, having some spells stay labeled as Solace creates inconsistency. Maybe it’s better to leave phrases like "draws on the existing mechanical profile of Deathknell of Solace" unchanged for now. But I’ll keep the user's request in mind.

**Rethinking the wording for Umbraxis**

I'm considering that if Umbraxis is indeed the god of Darkness, it seems like damage should lean more towards unholy or arcane rather than holy. The user specifically asked for a change in the wording of Solace, not the mechanics. I think maybe the trap's tone could fit better if it's termed as unholy. The chest trap "Judgment of the False Reliquary" could also be made unholy instead of holy. Replacing the pale blinding light with a smothering umbral flare sounds like a good idea, but it'll still need to allow for blindness.

**Refining mechanics and naming**

The user suggested a one-minute fixed time, and I think that might be the simplest approach. Still, it could get repetitive during exploration. For the Poisoned condition, maybe a fixed ten minutes isn’t necessary. I also considered changing the Bell-Wire effect to include both psychic and frightened conditions. As for naming, I wonder if "Judgment of the False Reliquary” should become “Benediction of Umbraxis.” I'm debating whether we should maintain a consistent grim style across the names.

**Aligning with darkness theme**

The user mentioned a god of Darkness, and that makes sense to incorporate into the naming. I think "Benediction of Umbraxis" is fitting for this effect. For the mechanics, how about a DC 13 PIE save that results in 3d8 unholy damage and causes the target to be Blinded for one minute? This aligns well with the darkness deity, and since unholy damage is canon, it seems great to implement. I'll go with that!

global changes**

The user might only want updates to the chest doc, but their request to "change all the Solace wording" suggests a broader change. Given the many references in the text, it could be wise to ask for clarification before doing a global replacement. However, if they prefer a global renaming, I need to be careful—certain phrases reflect significant lore changes. Automatic adjustments may work if they're switching to a god of Darkness, but it could get tricky with existing divine spells. I might suggest doing a broader pass later."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Clarifying the update request**

I’m considering mentioning that I’ve updated the trap file, but there are many other references to Solace in the repo. The user said "all the Solace wording," which makes me think they may want global changes. I’ll need to carefully check if I can edit the chest doc directly. It might be best to clarify their intent first. They usually want things done quickly, so I could offer to scope my changes to this document while noting the broader references for future updates. A global rename could be significant."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering name changes**

I need to think about renaming "Bell-Wire of Solace" to "Bell-Wire of Umbraxis" and updating authoring notes to reflect this. For "Judgment of the False Reliquary," maybe it can stay as it is if it doesn’t contain Solace wording. If I change everything to Umbraxis, I'd have to rename other spells to "Deathknell of Umbraxis" and "Judgment of Umbraxis." However, having some spells stay labeled as Solace creates inconsistency. Maybe it’s better to leave phrases like "draws on the existing mechanical profile of Deathknell of Solace" unchanged for now. But I’ll keep the user's request in mind."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Rethinking the wording for Umbraxis**

I'm considering that if Umbraxis is indeed the god of Darkness, it seems like damage should lean more towards unholy or arcane rather than holy. The user specifically asked for a change in the wording of Solace, not the mechanics. I think maybe the trap's tone could fit better if it's termed as unholy. The chest trap "Judgment of the False Reliquary" could also be made unholy instead of holy. Replacing the pale blinding light with a smothering umbral flare sounds like a good idea, but it'll still need to allow for blindness."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Refining mechanics and naming**

The user suggested a one-minute fixed time, and I think that might be the simplest approach. Still, it could get repetitive during exploration. For the Poisoned condition, maybe a fixed ten minutes isn’t necessary. I also considered changing the Bell-Wire effect to include both psychic and frightened conditions. As for naming, I wonder if "Judgment of the False Reliquary” should become “Benediction of Umbraxis.” I'm debating whether we should maintain a consistent grim style across the names."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Aligning with darkness theme**

The user mentioned a god of Darkness, and that makes sense to incorporate into the naming. I think "Benediction of Umbraxis" is fitting for this effect. For the mechanics, how about a DC 13 PIE save that results in 3d8 unholy damage and causes the target to be Blinded for one minute? This aligns well with the darkness deity, and since unholy damage is canon, it seems great to implement. I'll go with that!"}]}

arguments.oldText:
| 1 | **Ashen Sneeze-Wire** | Character opening | A puff of grave-dust bursts into the eyes and lungs. **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the condition. |
| 2 | **Taxman in the Lock** | Whole party | A gaunt, smiling revenant tallies the party's wealth with a bone stylus, then takes its due. **No save.** The party loses **10% of its carried gold**, rounded up. The chest may still be opened afterward if no other hazard remains. |
| 3 | **Bell-Wire of Solace** | Character opening | A tiny funeral bell tolls inside the skull. **DC 10 Piety save** or take **1d4 psychic** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, the target is also **Frightened** until the end of its next turn. |
| 4 | **Boneboiler's Greeting** | Character opening | A hidden bladder of hot grease bursts across the face and hands. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **2d4 fire** damage and become **Blinded** until the end of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |
| 5 | **Rotgas Latch** | Whole party | The lid exhales a sealed cloud of corpse-green boggart gas. Each party member must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Poisoned**. If a save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** until the end of their next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid both conditions. |

| 5 | **The Long Walk Home** | Whole party | The chest gives a single, hateful click. The floor, walls, and air fold like rotten cloth, and the party is hurled back to the **entrance or start** of the current dungeon. **No save.** All **non-magical light sources** currently burning are extinguished. |
| 6 | **Grave Slurry Reservoir** | Character opening | A hidden sac of corpse-sludge bursts over the arms and gear. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage and suffer a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check with **DC 5**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the item check. |
| 7 | **Gloamshadow Hand** | Character opening | A black, freezing grasp reaches out from inside the coffer and drinks the target's strength. **DC 12 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d3 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 8 | **Rotmother's Belch Jar** | Whole party | A sealed crock of bile, grease, and half-digested filth detonates across the party. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d6 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 9 | **Thorn-Coffin Mechanism** | Whole party | The chest blossoms into a cage of iron roots and inward-pointing thorns. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 + 2 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 10 | **Judgment of the False Reliquary** | Whole party | The chest erupts in a merciless pillar of pale, blinding light. - **Thornburst**
- **Judgment of Solace**

- **Griselda Rotmother** bile belch

## 3. Trap Tier A

These are cruel but mostly early-game chest hazards. They are meant to wound, panic, separate, or punish greed without making every trap an automatic death sentence.

| # | Trap Name | Target | Effect |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Ashen Sneeze-Wire** | Character opening | A puff of grave-dust bursts into the eyes and lungs. **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Blinded** for **1 minute**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the condition. |
| 2 | **Taxman in the Lock** | Whole party | A gaunt, smiling revenant tallies the party's wealth with a bone stylus, then takes its due. **No save.** The party loses **10% of its carried gold**, rounded up. The chest may still be opened afterward if no other hazard remains. |
| 3 | **Bell-Wire of Umbraxis** | Character opening | A tiny black funeral bell tolls inside the skull. **DC 10 Piety save** or take **1d4 psychic** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, the target is also **Frightened** for **1 minute**. |
| 4 | **Boneboiler's Greeting** | Character opening | A hidden bladder of hot grease bursts across the face and hands. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **2d4 fire** damage and become **Blinded** for **1 minute**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |
| 5 | **Rotgas Latch** | Whole party | The lid exhales a sealed cloud of corpse-green boggart gas. Each party member must make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or take **1d4 poison** damage and become **Poisoned**. If a save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** for **1 minute**. On a success, take half damage and avoid both conditions. |
| 6 | **Hook in the Velvet** | Character opening | A spring-hook punches through the wrist and tears free. **DC 11 Agility save** or take **1d6 piercing** damage and gain **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of the target's next turn. On a success, take half damage and avoid **Bleeding**. |
| 7 | **Thornburst Hinge** | Whole party | Blackened barbs and root-thorns explode outward from the chest seams. Each party member must make a **DC 11 Agility save** or take **2d4 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 8 | **Rotwing Nest** | Character opening | A starving rotwing bursts from a false coin layer and drives its proboscis deep. **DC 10 Agility save** or take **1d4 + 2 piercing** damage and gain **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of the target's next turn. If the hit lands, the target must then make a **DC 10 Constitution save** or contract **Veinrot** for **1 hour**. While affected by **Veinrot**, the target takes **50% more damage** from **Bleeding** effects. |
| 9 | **Gloamshadow Seal** | Character opening | A hand of cold dusk closes over the heart. **DC 11 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d2 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 10 | **Hungry Lid** | Character opening, then whole party | The chest splits along wet seams and becomes a **Chest Mimic**. The opener is immediately targeted by its first bite: **+5 to hit**, **1d10 + 3 piercing** damage, and the target must make a **DC 13 Constitution save** or become **Paralyzed**. After that strike, combat begins. The treasure is recovered if the mimic is slain. |

---

## 4. Trap Tier B

These traps are meant for stronger parties and nastier places. They lean harder into burst damage, permanent pressure, and effects that can turn a successful expedition into a crawl back to safety.

| # | Trap Name | Target | Effect |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Venomspit Ampoule** | Character opening | A glass ampoule snaps under the latch and spits corrosive venom upward. **DC 11 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 2 | **Poppet's Kiss** | Character opening | A spring-loaded needle punches into the flesh with doll-like precision. The target takes **1 piercing** damage, then must make a **DC 12 Constitution save** or take **2d4 poison** damage. On a success, the poison damage is halved. |
| 3 | **Rotgas Reliquary** | Whole party | A buried censer cracks open and floods the space with embalmed poison fog. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character is also **Blinded** for **1 minute**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the conditions. |
| 4 | **Arcbolt Cage** | Character opening | Copper wire and damp iron teeth snap shut in a crackling halo. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **3d4 lightning** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 5 | **The Long Walk Home** | Whole party | The chest gives a single, hateful click. The floor, walls, and air fold like rotten cloth, and the party is hurled back to the **entrance or start** of the current dungeon. **No save.** All **non-magical light sources** currently burning are extinguished. |
| 6 | **Grave Slurry Reservoir** | Character opening | A hidden sac of corpse-sludge bursts over the arms and gear. **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 acid** damage and suffer a **MINOR ITEM DESTRUCTION** check with **DC 5**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the item check. |
| 7 | **Gloamshadow Hand** | Character opening | A black, freezing grasp reaches out from inside the coffer and drinks the target's strength. **DC 12 Piety save** or take **1d6 cold** damage and lose **1d3 Strength** for **1 hour**. If this reduces the target's **Strength to 0**, the target dies. On a success, take half damage and avoid the Strength loss. |
| 8 | **Rotmother's Belch Jar** | Whole party | A sealed crock of bile, grease, and half-digested filth detonates across the party. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d6 acid** damage. On a success, take half damage. |
| 9 | **Thorn-Coffin Mechanism** | Whole party | The chest blossoms into a cage of iron roots and inward-pointing thorns. Each party member must make a **DC 12 Agility save** or take **2d4 + 2 piercing** damage. If the save fails by **5 or more**, that character also gains **Bleeding** for **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of their next turn. On a success, take half damage. |
| 10 | **Umbraxis' Black Benediction** | Whole party | The chest erupts in a devouring flare of black-violet radiance. Each party member must make a **DC 13 Piety save** or take **3d8 unholy** damage and become **Blinded** for **1 minute**. On a success, take half damage and avoid the blindness. |

---

## 5. Authoring Notes

The following trap entries deliberately draw on already-authored Umberhold material, including:
- existing divine bell-curse effects
- **Arcbolt**
- **Thornburst**
- existing divine judgment effects, re-skinned here for **Umbraxis**
- **Boggart Gasser** rotgas
- **Boggart Boneboiler** hot grease
- **Poppet of Pins** poison needle
- **Rotwing** bleeding and **Veinrot**
- **Gloamshadow** Strength-drain touch
- **Chest Mimic**
- **Grave Slurry** acid and item corrosion
- **Griselda Rotmother** bile belch

Changed:

- **Boneboiler's Greeting** blindness → **1 minute**
- **Rotgas Latch** blindness → **1 minute**
- **Rotgas Reliquary** blindness → **1 minute**
- Replaced the Solace-flavored Tier B capstone with:
  - **Umbraxis' Black Benediction**
  - now deals **unholy** damage
  - blindness lasts **1 minute**

One note:
- I only changed the **chest trap doc**.
- The wider Umberhold repo still has a lot of **Solace** references in spells, class docs, items, and dialogue. That’s a bigger lore rename pass.

If you want, next I can:
1. do a **full repo-wide Solace → Umbraxis pass**, or
2. do a **second timing pass** on the trap tables so all non-combat trap effects use exploration-friendly durations instead of any combat-style phrasing.
1. No, keep Solace for charcater spells. Since these traps are negative effects, it makes sense if we use an evil god as the name.

content.text:
That’s excellent. It gives the **Poppet of Pins** a much stronger identity.

What stands out from the art:
- **burlap / sackcloth body** rather than generic rag-doll cloth
- **thick black cord wrapping** around the torso and limbs
- a **stitched grin** rather than a blank sewn face
- **mismatched gemstone eyes** — one red, one green
- **long rusted nails/pins** bundled like claws
- **green venom** dripping from the pins
- **dangling control strings** trailing into the dark, which is a very creepy touch
- the crypt/tunnel setting makes it feel like a lurking ambush predator, not just a floating doll

I’d probably revise its description to better match this concept art like this:

> A small sackcloth doll, crudely stitched from filthy burlap and bound tight with black cord, hovers in the air with a jerking, puppet-like motion. Its mouth is sewn into a crooked grin, and two mismatched gemstone eyes—one red, one green—glimmer with dead, watchful malice. Long rusted nails jut from its hand in a fan of makeshift claws, each one wet with a slick green venom that drips to the floor below. Frayed strings trail from its limbs and back into the dark, as though some unseen hand still works it from beyond the torchlight. It does not walk. It twitches, drifts, and lunges, driving its poisoned pins into living flesh with horrible precision.

This also suggests a nice flavor detail:
- some Poppets may be found **near chests, nursery rooms, crypt corridors, or hanging from ceiling hooks and strings**
- multiple Poppets in the dark background implies they can appear in **clusters** or as a set-piece encounter

If you want, I can update `umberhold/monsters/poppet-of-pins.md` to match this concept art more closely.
